feed.no_news_desc: We couldn't find any articles. Try a different category or check back in a few minutes.
feed.heading_desc: Every article is instantly cross-referenced across trusted Indian publications, scored for accuracy by AI, and served in a distraction-free feed.

footer.description: India Verified is an open-source, AI-powered news verification platform that cross-references stories across trusted Indian publications in real time.
footer.sources_linked: Every claim links back to its sources
swipe.swipe_desc: Quickly browse through verified news stories. Swipe right to save, left to skip.
swipe.load_error_desc: We're having trouble reaching the server. Please try again.
swipe.break_desc: You've read {count} stories in a row. Taking short breaks helps you stay focused.
swipe.mobile_only_desc: Open this page on your phone to swipe through stories.
saved.page_desc: Bookmarks are stored on this device only — private and synced instantly across tabs.
saved.empty_desc: Tap the bookmark icon on any story to save it for later. Your reading list stays on this device.
saved.confirm_desc: This action cannot be undone. All your bookmarks will be permanently removed from this device.
saved.load_error: Could not load your saved stories.
search.title: Search Results
search.no_stories_desc: Try searching for different keywords or broader topics.
trending.title: Trending stories
cookie.title: Cookies & privacy
cookie.desc: We use minimal cookies to keep this site working.
cookie.body: We store a small number of strictly necessary cookies for core functionality — language preference, reading progress, and security. No tracking or advertising cookies are used.
common.error_body: We encountered an unexpected issue while loading this page. Try refreshing or return to the verified feed.
how_it_works.title: How India Verified works
how_it_works.subtitle: A fully automated, AI-powered news verification pipeline that runs 24/7 — no human editors needed.
how_it_works.step_1_desc: The pipeline starts by pulling news from dozens of trusted Indian RSS feeds and news APIs. Articles are deduplicated using SHA256 hashing, filtered through a domain blocklist to remove known satire and spam sources, and cross-checked against a database of known false claims.
how_it_works.step_2_desc: Each article group (2+ independent sources reporting the same story) is sent to Groq’s Llama 3.3 70B model for verification. The AI extracts key facts, assigns a 0–100 credibility score based on source authority, detail richness, and writing tone, and flags any red flags.
how_it_works.step_3_desc: Verified facts are rewritten into neutral, unbiased summaries. The AI generates a factual headline and concise summary that strips out sensationalism and presents the story as objectively as possible.
how_it_works.step_4_desc: The final verified article is inserted into the database and immediately appears on the website. Readers see the credibility score, source count, and links to all original articles for full transparency.
how_it_works.sources_desc: The pipeline aggregates from major Indian and international news outlets including Times of India, The Hindu, Hindustan Times, NDTV, Indian Express, BBC, Reuters, AP News, and many more. We also pull from Google News and GNews APIs for broader coverage.
how_it_works.scoring_desc: Every article receives a credibility score from 0–100. Scores above 65 are published. The score considers the Groq AI evaluation, number of independent sources, source authority, detail richness, and fact-check flags. Articles scoring below 65 are discarded with the reason logged.
how_it_works.open_source_desc: India Verified is fully open-source under the MIT licence. The entire codebase — frontend, pipeline, and infrastructure — is available on GitHub for audit, contribution, and reuse.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.desc: Your privacy matters. Here’s what data we store and why.
privacy.device_data_title: Data we store on your device
privacy.device_data_desc: India Verified stores small pieces of data in your browser to improve your experience. This includes your language preference, reading progress (scroll position per page), bookmarked stories, and swipe history. All of this stays on your device in localStorage and sessionStorage. We never upload or share this data with any server.
privacy.server_logs_desc: When you visit India Verified, our hosting provider (Vercel) automatically collects standard web server logs including your IP address, browser type, pages visited, and timestamps. These logs are retained temporarily for operational purposes and are not used for tracking or profiling. We do not use third-party analytics services.
privacy.cookies_desc: We use a minimal set of strictly necessary cookies. A language preference cookie stores your selected language (English or Hindi). A session cookie maintains reading progress. No tracking, advertising, or third-party cookies are used. You can clear these at any time through your browser settings.
privacy.children_desc: India Verified does not knowingly collect any personal information from children under 13. Our service is a general-audience news aggregator and does not target or appeal to children.
privacy.rights_desc: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to access, correct, or delete any personal data we hold. Since we store minimal data, most requests are automatically satisfied by clearing your browser data. For any questions, contact us at the email below.
privacy.changes_desc: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.contact_desc: For privacy-related inquiries, please open an issue on our GitHub repository or reach out to the maintainer directly through GitHub.
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.desc: Please read these terms carefully before using India Verified.
terms.what_is_desc: India Verified is an open-source, AI-powered news aggregator that cross-references stories across multiple trusted sources. It provides a single platform to consume verified news from dozens of Indian and international publications. The service is provided free of charge and without warranty.
terms.editorial_desc: While we strive for accuracy through automated AI verification, we make no guarantees about the completeness, accuracy, or reliability of any story. The AI verification system is a tool to assist readers, not a substitute for professional editorial judgment. Always verify critical information from primary sources.
terms.permitted_desc: You may access and read India Verified for personal, non-commercial use. You may share links to stories through standard sharing mechanisms. You may not scrape, republish, or redistribute the content without attribution. Automated access that imposes an unreasonable load on our infrastructure is prohibited.
terms.ip_desc: The India Verified codebase is open-source under the MIT licence. News content displayed on the platform is sourced from third-party publications and remains the intellectual property of the original publishers. The credibility scores, summaries, and verification metadata generated by the AI pipeline are provided under the same MIT licence as the codebase.
terms.warranties_desc: India Verified is provided “as is” without any warranty, express or implied. We do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation. The AI verification system may occasionally produce incorrect scores or miss inaccuracies in source reporting.
terms.liability_desc: In no event shall the maintainers or contributors of India Verified be liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use the service, including but not limited to reliance on AI-verified content.
terms.governing_desc: These terms are governed by the laws of India. Any disputes arising from these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in India.
terms.changes_desc: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated effective date. Continued use of India Verified after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
contact.title: Contact
contact.desc: Have a tip, correction, or feedback? We’d love to hear from you.
contact.story_tips_desc: If you come across a story that should be covered or a source we should add to our pipeline, please open an issue on our GitHub repository with the relevant links and context.
contact.corrections_desc: If you spot an error in a verified article — inaccurate summary, wrong category, miscited source — please visit our Corrections page to submit a report. Our team reviews every correction request.
contact.press_desc: For press inquiries, partnership opportunities, or syndication requests, please reach out through GitHub discussions or open an issue on the repository.
contact.privacy_legal_desc: For privacy-related questions or legal concerns, refer to our Privacy Policy and Terms of Service. For specific inquiries, open an issue on GitHub.
contact.feedback_desc: We welcome all feedback — feature suggestions, UI improvements, or general thoughts. The best place for this is our GitHub discussions or issues page.
corrections.title: Corrections
corrections.desc: We take accuracy seriously. Here’s how we handle errors.
corrections.what_is_title: What we treat as a correction
corrections.what_is_desc: A correction is any change to a published article that affects factual accuracy. This includes incorrect summaries, miscategorized stories, miscited sources, or outdated information that has been superseded by verified developments.
corrections.process_title: What happens after a correction is filed
corrections.process_desc: When a correction is submitted, the article’s status is updated to “corrected” and a correction note is appended. The original content remains visible with the correction clearly noted at the top. Readers can see the full correction history on each article.
corrections.retractions_desc: In cases where an article is found to be based on fundamentally incorrect information or fabricated sources, the article may be retracted entirely. Retracted articles remain visible with a clear “retracted” label and explanation, ensuring transparency.
corrections.report_desc: To report an error, open an issue on our GitHub repository with the article URL, a description of the error, and supporting evidence if available. Each report is reviewed by the maintainers. For urgent corrections, please mention the issue directly in the repository.
corrections.wont_change_desc: We do not alter articles for differences of opinion or editorial preference. Corrections are strictly for factual inaccuracies. Credibility scores may be updated if new information emerges, but this is noted transparently.
